title: calorophus vestitus
intro: Calorophus vestitus, commonly known as the Mexican Goldbird flower or Calostemma, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Iridaceae. This plant is known for its vibrant yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Calorophus vestitus include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic actions, such as wound healing, antispasmodic, and antioxidant effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits and therapeutic actions.
side_effects: Calorophus vestitus can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach pain due to the high concentration of alkaloids in its leaves. In severe cases, it can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and dehydration if ingested in large amounts. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can also cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering. Improper use of Calorophus vestitus can even lead to respiratory problems, such as coughing and shortness of breath, especially for individuals with pre-existing lung conditions.
